How's it going everyone? In this video we're going to cover creating the Facebook group that you're going to use for this play. This Facebook group is going to be a great central place for everybody participating to be able to share their successes, support each other, and just get everybody hyped up. It's also a great place to keep track of how to get in touch with the people participating over Facebook as well. If you already are familiar with how to create a Facebook group, how to change the announcement, how to cover photo, invite people to it, approve posts and stories, you can probably skip this. Otherwise follow along and we'll walk you through step by step. Go ahead and get logged into the Facebook account that you want to use as the admin for this group, the main account. On this left hand side you want to find groups. Now if you have a bunch of groups or apps installed you may have to scroll down a bit to see it, but under explore you should see groups as a little icon with the people on it. Go ahead and click on that and it's going to take you to groups. If you have a whole bunch of groups that you're a part of you're going to see them listed here. If you don't that's okay. At the top right you want to look for this green create group button. Once you click on that green create group at the top right, it's going to open this box where you can name your group. We're going to choose a group name that's very specific to this so that way you know exactly which one it is. Now whenever you create a group you do have to invite at least one person to it. So this would be a great time to invite one of your managers or trainers. It's going to be a part of this challenge so that way you have somebody else who can help you monitor and manage it as well. So let's go ahead and add in somebody and again at least one person has to be there. Right now it's set to close group. Anyone can find the group and see who runs it but only members can see who's in it and what they post. We're going to go ahead and click on that and go ahead and change it to seeker group. Only members can find the group see who's in it and what they post. That way you can keep things restricted. Either one is fine. It's just up to you whether or not you want people to be able to actually see this group. If this looks good go ahead and hit the blue create button. Now one thing to note is this pin to shortcuts over here. If you put a check mark there that means it's going to put it at the top of the list once you log into Facebook. That way it's easy to find this group and while this challenge is active I highly recommend you do that. That way it's always in the front of your mind when you look at Facebook and you remember to check it and make sure you're taking care of it. Everything looks good here. Let's go ahead and hit this blue create button. It'll take a couple moments and then take you to your new group. You can see the name over there and you can start making changes. One of the first things you want to do to personalize your group is upload a photo to it. So I'm going to hit this upload photo and I'm going to go and put in a logo. Now you can drag it around to reposition if you need to and then hit save. Of course yours is going to look a lot better than that I hope. We also want to do a welcome announcement. This will be the first post. It's always at the top and it tells people what's going on. To make an announcement you make a post and then you set that post as the announcement. So on the main page of your group the discussion tab where it says write something that's where you'll put your post at. You want to make sure to include a good positive message. You want to include the challenge dates when the challenge starts and the way out all of the relevant information that they need to know. Once you get that post in there and I'm just going to put in something quick make sure yours is pretty informational you can use lots of emojis to hype them up. Once you have that post look for it and then right there there is a drop down arrow click on that arrow and choose mark as announcement. So that's going to make that post the first one that everybody sees when they get to the group. So that's all it is to make an announcement. So now that announcement post even if other people post the announcements always going to be at the top. The next one we're going to look at is members. If you look on this menu on the left you have members go ahead and click on that. Now members is where you can see anybody who's a member of your group. You can see the post that they make with this view recent post link right there and you can also remove them from the group or make them a moderator. So so you have a trainer that's helping you out with this or you have somebody who's left the challenge you can change it there. So you just want to look for the person that you need to make changes to and to the right of their name will be the three dots. When you click on that it gives you the option to change it to moderator or leave group. Now since I'm the admin and this is my account that's why you see that remove as admin. Normally you never want to remove as admin because that means you lose the overall control of the group. So always focus on this change to moderator or leave group and make sure you're making only people you really trust the moderators because they'll be able to control the posts and who joins it. So it's best to only give that to people who are actually employed by your gym and participating as part of this challenge or if you have somebody who handles your social media maybe you want to make them a moderator. Here under all members you can remove them from the group too. You don't see that since we don't have other people here but that's going to be an option there once you get other people joining too. So that's how you get rid of somebody if you need to. On this left hand side is moderate group. I'm not going to go and click on that. This gives you a chance to go through all of the different options with it. So we have admin activity which shows anything you've recently done. You have scheduled posts so if you want to make it to where automatically post something later on you can add a scheduled post in here or at least see and manage them. Whenever somebody requests to join the group because you're going to be providing the link to this group to people whenever they request it you'll see it under member requests and you'll be able to approve or deny those. Approval notifications will set the settings for letting you know if somebody gets notified or requests to do it. I always recommend to leave this as get notifications that way you can respond as quick as possible. You can set up rules now we did put some of the rules inside of the announcement post but if you want to put your rules here you can set them up under this create rules as well. Right here where it says member reported whenever somebody reports a post or a story or a comment it's going to show up here that way you can take a look at it see what's going on. And here we have auto flagged that means that Facebook automatically detected that whatever this post or comment or story was did not meet their standards. It's going to pop here automatically hopefully you'll never have to deal with that. So I'm going to go back to the group by clicking the group name right there. I'm going to click this more right here and so here we can add members so if you want to add somebody directly you can go to moderate group which is that same as right there but we're going to go to edit group settings so let's go ahead and hit edit group settings. If you want to give a type of your group go ahead and do that since this is a private group you don't have to worry about that group type is really just for helping people find your group. The same is for description it lets people know what your group is about if you want them to be able to find them. For location I do recommend adding a location and choosing your gym in here that way they know where to meet up in case they ever forget. If you want to change your group between secret or public or private you can click that change privacy make the change and hit confirm. We're going to leave it as secret for now but you can change that to closed if you need to or if you chose the wrong one before that's how you can fix it. Membership approval that means anybody in the group is able to approve people joining the group normally you don't want that so I'm going to change this to only admins and moderators are able to approve new members. Chat is normally fun to leave for anybody to create. Membership pre-approvals lets you pre-approve people with their email address or if they're in another group you don't have to do that it's just a kind of shortcut if you have their email addresses you can do that pre-approval process but you are also going to be sending them the link to this group directly and they'll be able to request to join from there. Posting permissions is fun as anyone in the group I do recommend setting post approval to a checkmark that means any post that people make inside of your group have to be approved before they're able to be seen that's a great way to kind of create a little bit of a wall and in case somebody posts something that you really wouldn't want others to be involved with you can prevent that before it ever even pops up for them. Story posting permissions anyone in the group is fine and then story post approval we do want to put a checkmark there it's the same thing as post approval it's just for stories you want to make sure that you get a chance to review it before allowing it to go to everybody in the group. So the only real options we changed in here we put a checkmark next to post approval and a checkmark next to story post approval this looks great so I'm going to go ahead and hit this blue save button and it lets you know that your changes have been saved. Now I want to show you how to get the link for this group that way you have it to share later on if you click right here on your group title at the top left it'll take you back to the main page and up here at the top left in the address bar that URL that link that's the one that you use to go directly to this group. You want to select it all so you can click and drag with your mouse and copy it to your clipboard. If you're using a tracking document you can take that link and put that right in the tracking document that way you have it all in one place otherwise you can go ahead and share it with other people either email it put in your click funnel whatever you need to do and you can always get that link again by coming back to facebook opening up the group and then you'll see that link up at the top. Hope you enjoyed this video and hope you have a great rest of your day.
